note: No physical constants. 'Verification' for this claim is (a) structural consistency of GCM's Bell-escape choice, and (b) a machine-readable bibliography for every load-bearing citation in the markdown. Speed-of-Causality Principle not engaged here.

logical_variables.Lo: Locality — no faster-than-light signaling between spacelike-separated events.
logical_variables.Re: Realism — hidden variables have definite values independent of measurement.
logical_variables.MI: Measurement Independence — experimenter's setting choice is uncorrelated with hidden-variable state λ; i.e. ρ(λ | a, b) = ρ(λ).
logical_variables.BI: Bell inequality satisfied (prediction of Lo ∧ Re ∧ MI).
logical_variables.QM: Quantum-mechanical correlations observed (experimentally confirmed; violate BI).

bell_theorem.consequence: ¬(Lo ∧ Re ∧ MI) — at least one of {Lo, Re, MI} must fail.

gcm_commitment.justification: GCM's minimal model is fully deterministic. Correlations between λ and measurement choices (a, b) are common-history correlations, not FTL signaling.
gcm_commitment.nearest_peer_reviewed_precedent: 't Hooft 2016, Cellular Automaton Interpretation of Quantum Mechanics

citations.cited_text: Gerard 't Hooft's Cellular Automaton Interpretation of Quantum Mechanics (Springer, 2016)
author: 't Hooft, Gerard
citations.title: The Cellular Automaton Interpretation of Quantum Mechanics
citations.venue: Springer, Fundamental Theories of Physics, Vol. 185
citations.load_bearing_for: Nearest-neighbor peer-reviewed precedent for GCM's superdeterministic commitment.

author: Bell, J. S.
citations.title: On the Einstein Podolsky Rosen paradox
citations.load_bearing_for: Original proof that no local-hidden-variable theory with measurement independence reproduces QM.

citations.cited_text: Hossenfelder and Palmer, 'Rethinking Superdeterminism,' Frontiers in Physics, 2020
author: Hossenfelder, Sabine
Palmer, Tim N.
citations.title: Rethinking Superdeterminism
citations.load_bearing_for: Modern defence of superdeterminism; treats testability and conspiracy objections.

citations.cited_text: experiment has confirmed quantum mechanics on this point (Aspect 1982; Hensen et al. 2015 loophole-free)
